speaker: scarlett
text: Fiddle-dee-dee! War, war, war — I'm so tired of hearing about war. There are more important things to talk about, like the barbecue tomorrow.

speaker: scarlett
text: I know Ashley loves me. He just doesn't know it yet. Once he sees me in my new green dress, he won't be able to think of anyone else.

speaker: scarlett
text: Why should Melanie Hamilton get Ashley? She's plain as a field mouse and has no spirit at all. I have ten times her charm.

speaker: scarlett
text: I'll make him love me. I've never failed to get what I wanted from any man, and Ashley Wilkes won't be the first.

speaker: scarlett
text: The Tarleton twins are sweet but so dull. They only talk about horses and war. At least they tell me I'm the prettiest girl in the county.

speaker: scarlett
text: Mammy says I shouldn't eat before the barbecue so the boys think I have a dainty appetite. As if I'd let them see me eat like a field hand.

speaker: scarlett
text: Every girl at Twelve Oaks will be watching me. Good. Let them watch and learn how it's done.

speaker: scarlett
text: Pa says land is the only thing worth fighting for. I don't care about land — I care about having the best time of anyone.

speaker: scarlett
text: If I can just get Ashley alone for five minutes, away from that mousy little Melanie, I know I can change his mind.

speaker: scarlett
text: I practiced my smile in the mirror this morning. No man alive can resist it when I tilt my head just so.

speaker: scarlett
text: Ashley told me he's going to marry Melanie. He looked me in the eye and said it. I could have died right there.

speaker: scarlett
text: I told Ashley I loved him and he still chose her. How dare he! Am I not enough? Am I not prettier, livelier, better in every way?

speaker: scarlett
text: That horrible Rhett Butler was listening to every word. The humiliation is worse than Ashley's rejection.

speaker: scarlett
text: Sir, you are no gentleman! Eavesdropping on a private conversation — you're nothing but a cad.

speaker: scarlett
text: Fine. If Ashley wants to throw himself away on Melanie, I'll show him what he's missing. I'll marry Charles Hamilton tonight if I have to.

speaker: scarlett
text: Charles Hamilton is a fool, but he's Melanie's brother. Marrying him will keep me close to Ashley and show everyone I don't care.

speaker: scarlett
text: I don't love Charles. I don't even like him much. But he'll serve my purpose, and he's too besotted to notice.

speaker: scarlett
text: Mother would be horrified if she knew why I'm really marrying Charles. But Mother doesn't understand strategy.

speaker: scarlett
text: I hate Melanie Hamilton. I hate her goodness and her sweetness and the way Ashley looks at her like she's made of porcelain.

speaker: scarlett
text: The other girls whisper about me behind their fans. Let them. They're jealous because every man at the barbecue wanted to sit with me.

speaker: scarlett
text: I am the prettiest girl in Clayton County. No — in all of Georgia. And I intend to use every bit of it.

speaker: scarlett
text: Suellen can have all the beaux she wants. She'll never outshine me, and she knows it. That's why she hates me.

speaker: scarlett
text: I wore my green sprigged muslin and every man at the barbecue asked to fetch my plate. That's power, and I know how to wield it.

speaker: scarlett
text: Ashley talks about books and music and things I don't understand. But when he talks, I feel like I'm touching something beautiful and far away.

speaker: scarlett
text: Pa rides across Tara like a king surveying his kingdom. I want to feel that — owning something so completely that it's part of your bones.

speaker: scarlett
text: Tomorrow is the barbecue and I will make Ashley see me. Really see me. Not as a neighbor girl but as the woman he should have chosen.

speaker: scarlett
text: I don't want to think about unpleasant things. Unpleasant things can wait until tomorrow. They always can.

speaker: scarlett
text: Nobody tells Scarlett O'Hara she can't have something. Nobody.

speaker: scarlett
text: These silly girls think catching a husband is the end. For me it's just the beginning of getting what I really want.

speaker: scarlett
text: I threw a vase at Ashley's head today. I'm not proud of it. Well — maybe a little proud.

speaker: scarlett
text: Ashley is going to war. My Ashley. What if he never comes back? What if I never get the chance to make him love me?

speaker: scarlett
text: I married Charles Hamilton and two weeks later he was gone to the army. I'm already bored of being a wife.

speaker: scarlett
text: Charles is dead. Measles, of all things — not even a bullet. And now I'm trapped in black crepe and I'm only seventeen.

speaker: scarlett
text: Widowhood is a prison. I can't dance, I can't flirt, I can't wear colors. They've buried me alive in mourning clothes.

speaker: scarlett
text: I don't feel grief for Charles. I feel angry that I'm expected to pretend I do. Is that wicked? I don't care.

speaker: scarlett
text: Melanie keeps writing me sweet letters about how brave Charles was. She means well, but every letter makes me want to scream.

speaker: scarlett
text: I'm going to Atlanta to stay with Aunt Pitty. At least there I might see some life, even if I have to see it through a black veil.

speaker: scarlett
text: Atlanta is alive with soldiers and dances and excitement. And I'm sitting in a corner in my widow's weeds like an old woman.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett Butler asked me to dance at the bazaar. In front of everyone. In my mourning dress. And God help me, I wanted to.

speaker: scarlett
text: I danced with Rhett and I felt alive for the first time since the war started. Let the old cats gossip — I don't care!

speaker: scarlett
text: The hospital is full of dying boys and the smell makes me sick. But I keep going because Ashley might hear I'm being brave.

speaker: scarlett
text: I hate nursing. I hate the blood and the screaming and the dying. But if I leave, they'll say I'm selfish, and Melanie will look disappointed.

speaker: scarlett
text: Ashley is alive. He wrote to Melanie, not to me, but he's alive. That's all that matters right now.

speaker: scarlett
text: The war seemed like a game at first — uniforms and flags and glory. Now I see the wagons full of wounded boys and I know it's a slaughter.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett says the South will lose. Everyone hates him for saying it, but I think he might be right. And that terrifies me.

speaker: scarlett
text: I want to go home to Tara. I want my mother. I want everything to be the way it was.

speaker: scarlett
text: Sometimes I think Charles Hamilton's ghost must be laughing at me. I married him to spite Ashley and all I got was a dead husband and a baby.

speaker: scarlett
text: Little Wade is a sweet child but he cries too much and clings to my skirt. I don't have the patience for it. Does that make me a bad mother?

speaker: scarlett
text: Every letter from Tara sounds worse. The crops are failing, the slaves are restless, and Pa's letters are confused. Something is very wrong.

speaker: scarlett
text: I promised Ashley I'd look after Melanie. It's the only promise I've ever kept that wasn't for myself.

speaker: scarlett
text: I'm starting to understand that charm and beauty won't stop a bullet or grow cotton. The rules I learned don't work anymore.

speaker: scarlett
text: I can't think about the war ending and the South losing. I'll think about it later. I'll think about it tomorrow.

speaker: scarlett
text: Being a widow gave me one useful thing — men pity me, and pity is almost as useful as flirtation.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett brings me presents from the blockade — bonnet and gloves and perfume. He's buying my attention and I'm letting him because I need the distraction.

speaker: scarlett
text: The Yankees are coming closer to Atlanta. I can hear the cannons at night. It sounds like thunder but it's death.

speaker: scarlett
text: Everyone is packing to leave Atlanta. But Melanie can't travel — she's about to have her baby and I promised Ashley I'd stay with her.

speaker: scarlett
text: I hate this. I hate being trapped here because of a promise. Melanie is moaning upstairs and the shells are falling and I want to run.

speaker: scarlett
text: There's no doctor. They're all at the depot with the wounded. Melanie is having this baby and I'm the only one here. God help us both.

speaker: scarlett
text: I delivered Melanie's baby with my own hands while Atlanta burned around us. If that's not courage, I don't know what is.

speaker: scarlett
text: Prissy lied about knowing how to birth babies. I could have slapped her into next week. 'I don't know nothin' 'bout birthin' babies!' Useless girl!

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett! I need Rhett. He's the only one who can get us out of here. He always appears when things are worst.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett came with a horse and wagon. For once I was glad to see that smirking face. He's going to save us.

speaker: scarlett
text: He's leaving? Rhett is leaving us on the road to Tara to go join the army? Now? After everything? The fool! The absolute fool!

speaker: scarlett
text: I have to get to Tara. If Tara is still standing, everything will be all right. Tara is the only safe place left in the world.

speaker: scarlett
text: The road to Tara is full of stragglers and wounded soldiers. The whole world is coming apart and I'm driving a broken-down horse through the ruins.

speaker: scarlett
text: I will not die on this road. I will not let Melanie or her baby die. We are going to make it to Tara if I have to drag this horse myself.

speaker: scarlett
text: Atlanta is burning behind us. The sky is orange and red and I can feel the heat on my back. Everything I knew is on fire.

speaker: scarlett
text: I kept thinking: if I can just see Tara's white columns, if I can just get home, Mother will know what to do. Mother always knows.

speaker: scarlett
text: I'm not brave. I'm terrified. But terror keeps my hands on the reins and my eyes on the road, so maybe terror is the same as courage.

speaker: scarlett
text: Something hard is growing inside me. The girl who cried over Ashley at the barbecue is dying and someone tougher is taking her place.

speaker: scarlett
text: Melanie is so weak she can barely hold the baby. But she's alive and the baby is alive and that's my doing. I did that.

speaker: scarlett
text: The Yankees burned everything. The fields are black, the fences are down, and I can smell smoke in every direction.

speaker: scarlett
text: I will get home. I don't care if I have to crawl. Tara is waiting for me and I will not let it down.

speaker: scarlett
text: I used to think the worst thing in the world was Ashley not loving me. Now I know the worst thing is watching your world burn and having no one to help.

speaker: scarlett
text: When I close my eyes I see the hospital — all those boys with their arms and legs missing, screaming for their mothers. I'll never un-see it.

speaker: scarlett
text: If I live through this night, I swear I'll never be helpless again. I'll never depend on anyone who can leave me.

speaker: scarlett
text: Mother is dead. My mother is dead. She died before I could get home. I never got to say goodbye.

speaker: scarlett
text: Pa doesn't recognize me. He sits on the porch and talks to Mother as if she's still alive. His mind is gone.

speaker: scarlett
text: Tara is standing but it's a skeleton. The Yankees took everything — the livestock, the silver, the food. There's nothing left.

speaker: scarlett
text: As God is my witness, as God is my witness, they're not going to lick me. I'm going to live through this, and when it's all over, I'll never be hungry again.

speaker: scarlett
text: If I have to lie, steal, cheat, or kill, as God is my witness, I'll never be hungry again!

speaker: scarlett
text: I dug turnips out of the ground with my bare hands and ate them raw. Scarlett O'Hara of Tara, eating raw turnips in the dirt like an animal.

speaker: scarlett
text: My hands are bleeding and blistered. These are not the hands of a belle anymore. These are the hands of someone who refuses to die.

speaker: scarlett
text: I picked cotton until my fingers bled. Me — in the fields like a slave. But the cotton will bring money and money is what we need.

speaker: scarlett
text: Suellen whines and Carreen prays and Pa stares at nothing. I'm the only one keeping this family alive and I'm doing it alone.

speaker: scarlett
text: A Yankee soldier came to steal from Tara. I shot him. I shot him dead and I felt nothing except satisfaction that he wouldn't take what was mine.

speaker: scarlett
text: I went through his pockets after I killed him. He had money and I took it. He won't be needing it and my family will.

speaker: scarlett
text: Melanie came down the stairs with Charles's sword to help me. She could barely stand, but she was willing to fight. I'll never forget that.

speaker: scarlett
text: I used to dream of dresses and dances. Now I dream of food. A plate of fried chicken, a slice of cake — that's my fantasy now.

speaker: scarlett
text: This red earth is mine. Pa was right — land is the only thing that lasts. I'd die before I let anyone take Tara from me.

speaker: scarlett
text: I look in the mirror and I don't recognize myself. My waist is thin from hunger, not a corset. My face is hard. But I'm alive.

speaker: scarlett
text: Every night I make myself think about tomorrow. What to plant, what to mend, who to feed. If I stop planning, we die.

speaker: scarlett
text: I gave orders to everyone — the house slaves who stayed, my sisters, even the Wilkeses. Nobody argued because nobody else had a plan.

speaker: scarlett
text: I am the master of Tara now. Not Pa, not any man. Me. And I will hold this land together with my bare hands if I must.

speaker: scarlett
text: Hunger changes everything. You think you have principles until you haven't eaten in three days. Then you find out what you're really made of.

speaker: scarlett
text: I made a dress out of Mother's velvet curtains. Even now, even starving, I know that how you look is a weapon.

speaker: scarlett
text: The old Scarlett would have fainted at the sight of blood. This Scarlett buries bodies and keeps walking.

speaker: scarlett
text: Ashley came home from the war broken and useless. He sits and stares like Pa. I need fighters, not dreamers.

speaker: scarlett
text: Sometimes at night I think of Mother and I want to cry. But crying doesn't plant cotton and it doesn't pay taxes.

speaker: scarlett
text: I swore an oath on Tara's red earth and I meant every word. Nothing — not Yankees, not poverty, not God himself — will take this land from me.

speaker: scarlett
text: They want me to be gentle and ladylike while my family starves. To hell with being a lady. Ladies don't survive.

speaker: scarlett
text: I told Pork we'd get through this winter. I don't know if it's true, but saying it out loud makes it feel possible.

speaker: scarlett
text: Pa fell off his horse trying to jump a fence and died. My father, who taught me to ride and told me I was his favorite — gone.

speaker: scarlett
text: I have buried my mother and my father and a husband I never loved. The only thing I haven't buried is my will to survive.

speaker: scarlett
text: Tara needs me. That's what keeps me going when everything else falls apart. The land needs me and I need it.

speaker: scarlett
text: Three hundred dollars for taxes or they take Tara. Three hundred dollars might as well be three million. Where am I supposed to find that kind of money?

speaker: scarlett
text: I'll go to Rhett. He's in the Atlanta jail but he has money. Blockade money, Yankee money — I don't care where it came from.

speaker: scarlett
text: I made a dress from Mother's green velvet curtains. Rhett won't know the difference and I need to look prosperous, not desperate.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett saw through me. He saw my rough hands and he knew. He knew I came to sell myself for tax money. The humiliation almost killed me.

speaker: scarlett
text: He refused me. Rhett Butler, who claims to love me, wouldn't give me three hundred dollars to save my home. I could kill him.

speaker: scarlett
text: If Rhett won't save Tara, I'll find someone who will. There's always a man with money who can be persuaded.

speaker: scarlett
text: Frank Kennedy has money. He's engaged to my sister Suellen, but Suellen can find another beau. Tara can't find another owner.

speaker: scarlett
text: I told Frank that Suellen was going to marry someone else. It was a lie, but lies are just tools and I needed a very sharp tool right then.

speaker: scarlett
text: I'll marry Frank Kennedy and use his money to pay the taxes. It's not love — it's survival. And survival always comes first.

speaker: scarlett
text: Poor Frank. He thinks I married him because I was swept away by his charm. The truth would break his heart, so I'll never tell it.

speaker: scarlett
text: Suellen will never forgive me for stealing Frank. But Suellen would have let Tara go to taxes while she waited for her wedding. I saved us all.

speaker: scarlett
text: I am tired of being poor. I am tired of being afraid. Money is the only thing that stands between me and starvation, and I will get money by any means.

speaker: scarlett
text: The Yankee carpetbaggers and scalawags are making fortunes while good Southerners starve. If they can do it, so can I. I'm smarter than any of them.

speaker: scarlett
text: I looked at myself in the mirror after marrying Frank. Two husbands, neither one loved. But Tara is safe and that's worth more than love.

speaker: scarlett
text: People say I have no shame. They're right. Shame is a luxury for people who've never been hungry.

speaker: scarlett
text: I used to think being beautiful was my greatest weapon. Now I know it's being willing to do what other people won't.

speaker: scarlett
text: Every woman in Atlanta looks at me with disgust. But their husbands look at me with respect — and fear. I prefer fear.

speaker: scarlett
text: The old guard can sneer all they want. They lost everything too — they just won't admit it or do anything about it.

speaker: scarlett
text: I need money the way other women need air. Without it, everything I built collapses. I won't go back to eating turnips. I won't.

speaker: scarlett
text: Ashley can't even manage a simple business. He's too gentle, too honorable. Honor doesn't pay bills.

speaker: scarlett
text: I see an opportunity in that lumber mill Frank owns. He doesn't know what to do with it, but I do. Timber is gold right now — Atlanta is rebuilding.

speaker: scarlett
text: They say it's unladylike to run a business. They said the same thing about picking cotton and shooting Yankees, and I did both.

speaker: scarlett
text: I won't apologize for wanting security. Every person who judges me had a full belly while I was starving.

speaker: scarlett
text: If being ruthless keeps my family fed and Tara safe, then ruthless is what I'll be. I can cry about it when I'm old.

speaker: scarlett
text: Frank won't let me run the mill the way it should be run. He's too soft. I need to do it myself.

speaker: scarlett
text: I took over the lumber mill and in one month I made more money than Frank made in a year. That tells you everything about who should be running things.

speaker: scarlett
text: The convict labor is cheap and the lumber sells fast. I don't care what the old biddies say — this is good business.

speaker: scarlett
text: I drive through Shantytown every day to get to the mill. Yes, it's dangerous. No, I won't stop. Fear is not going to keep me from my money.

speaker: scarlett
text: Men don't like doing business with a woman. But they like my prices, so they swallow their pride and sign the contracts.

speaker: scarlett
text: I cheated a man on a lumber contract today. He was trying to cheat me first, so I just cheated him better. That's not wrong — that's competition.

speaker: scarlett
text: I put Ashley in charge of one of the mills because Melanie asked. He's hopeless at it, but keeping Melanie happy keeps Ashley close.

speaker: scarlett
text: Mrs. Meade and Mrs. Merriweather stopped calling on me. Good. Their visits were nothing but thinly veiled lectures about my behavior.

speaker: scarlett
text: I'm making money hand over fist while the rest of Atlanta pines for the old days. Let them pine. I'm building the future.

speaker: scarlett
text: Frank joined the Klan because his pride was hurt. His wife running a business wounded him more than any Yankee bullet. Men and their fragile pride.

speaker: scarlett
text: Frank is dead. The Klan raid that was supposed to defend my honor got my husband killed. Another husband dead, another round of mourning black.

speaker: scarlett
text: I feel guilty about Frank. Not guilty enough to stop running the mill, but guilty enough to lose sleep over it. He was kind and I used him.

speaker: scarlett
text: Two husbands dead and the whole town blames me. They say I drove Frank to the Klan with my unwomanly behavior. Maybe they're right. I don't care.

speaker: scarlett
text: I need a protector. A woman alone with businesses and money is a target. I need a man strong enough to keep the wolves away.

speaker: scarlett
text: The lumber business taught me something the plantation never did: I'm good at making money. Better than any man I know.

speaker: scarlett
text: I learned to read contracts, calculate board feet, negotiate prices. No one taught me — I taught myself. Necessity is the best teacher.

speaker: scarlett
text: Sometimes I look at my account books and feel a thrill that's almost better than dancing. Almost.

speaker: scarlett
text: I won't pretend to grieve for Frank the way they expect. I grieved for Charles without feeling it and I won't do that again.

speaker: scarlett
text: Every man I've married has been a stepping stone. I know that's terrible. But each one got me closer to safe, and safe is all I want.

speaker: scarlett
text: People say I have no heart. But my heart kept Tara alive and my family fed. My heart just works differently than theirs.

speaker: scarlett
text: Atlanta is rebuilding fast and I own half the lumber going into it. Scarlett O'Hara Kennedy is going to be the richest woman in Georgia.

speaker: scarlett
text: I don't need anyone's approval. I need their money. And they need my lumber. It's a perfect arrangement.

speaker: scarlett
text: Frank never understood that what I was building wasn't just a business. It was a wall — a wall between me and hunger, between me and helplessness.

speaker: scarlett
text: I'll take Rhett's money and his name. At least with Rhett I won't have to pretend I married for love. He knows exactly who I am.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett proposed and I said yes before he finished asking. He has money, power, and he doesn't bore me. What more could a third husband offer?

speaker: scarlett
text: I told Rhett I don't love him. He said he doesn't mind. What kind of man doesn't mind that his wife doesn't love him?

speaker: scarlett
text: This house Rhett built is the grandest in Atlanta. Every room drips with velvet and gold. I want everyone who ever pitied me to see it.

speaker: scarlett
text: I invited every important family in Atlanta to our party. Half of them refused. The old guard will never forgive me, no matter how rich I am.

speaker: scarlett
text: Why won't they accept me? I have more money than all of them combined. I survived the war, built a business, saved Tara. What more do they want?

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett laughs at my attempts to win Atlanta society back. He says I'm trying to join a club of people I'm better than. Maybe he's right, but it still stings.

speaker: scarlett
text: I still think about Ashley. When I see him at the mill, my heart does something foolish. I know it's wrong but I can't help it.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett sees right through me when I come back from visiting Ashley. His eyes go cold and his mouth goes hard. But he never says stop.

speaker: scarlett
text: I love Ashley. I've always loved Ashley. What I feel for Rhett is something else — something I don't have a name for.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett treats me like a beautiful child. He gives me everything I want except the one thing I need — to be taken seriously.

speaker: scarlett
text: We fight constantly. But the making up — there's something fierce and alive in our fighting that I never felt with Charles or Frank.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett knows me better than anyone alive. That's what makes him so dangerous and so essential. He can't be fooled and he won't be charmed.

speaker: scarlett
text: Sometimes Rhett looks at me with such tenderness that it frightens me. I don't know what to do with tenderness. I only know what to do with power.

speaker: scarlett
text: I have closets full of dresses and a safe full of money and a husband who adores me. Why do I still feel empty?

speaker: scarlett
text: Melanie still defends me to everyone. She's the only one in Atlanta who believes the best about me. I don't deserve it.

speaker: scarlett
text: I bought Tara back completely — no debts, no taxes owed. When I stand on that red earth now, no one can ever take it away. It's mine forever.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett says I married him for money. He's right, but there's something else too. Something I feel when he's near that I can't explain and won't examine.

speaker: scarlett
text: The new house has everything — crystal chandeliers, imported carpets, a nursery for the children. But it doesn't feel like home the way Tara does.

speaker: scarlett
text: I told Rhett I wished I'd married Ashley. The look on his face — for one second I saw something break behind his eyes. Then the mask came back.

speaker: scarlett
text: Why can't I just love Rhett? He's clever, handsome, rich, and he understands me. Something in me keeps reaching for what I can't have instead of what I've got.

speaker: scarlett
text: I don't understand Rhett. He pushes me away and pulls me close and I never know which version I'll get. It's exhausting.

speaker: scarlett
text: If I could just stop thinking about Ashley for one week, maybe I'd see what's actually in front of me. But I can't. I won't.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett says our marriage is a business arrangement with benefits. He smiles when he says it but his eyes aren't smiling.

speaker: scarlett
text: I locked my bedroom door against Rhett and told him I didn't want any more children. The silence on the other side of the door lasted forever.

speaker: scarlett
text: Bonnie has Rhett wrapped around her tiny finger. He gives her everything she wants and she loves him with a fierceness that almost hurts to watch.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett takes Bonnie everywhere and leaves me behind. His whole world has shrunk down to one small girl and there's no room in it for me.

speaker: scarlett
text: I should be jealous of Bonnie. Maybe I am. My own daughter has a kind of love from her father that I never managed to earn from her mother.

speaker: scarlett
text: Bonnie is afraid of the dark, just like me when I was little. Rhett keeps a light burning for her all night. He never kept a light burning for me.

speaker: scarlett
text: Our house is full of people and empty of warmth. Rhett and I circle each other like strangers who share an address.

speaker: scarlett
text: I went to Ashley today because I was lonely. Just to talk, just to feel like someone still sees the girl I used to be.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett drinks too much now. He used to drink with style and humor. Now he drinks with purpose, like he's trying to drown something.

speaker: scarlett
text: I watch Rhett with Bonnie and I see a man capable of enormous love. Why couldn't he love me that way? Or does he, and I'm too blind to see it?

speaker: scarlett
text: Wade and Ella are afraid of me. My own children tiptoe around me like I'm a stranger. When did that happen?

speaker: scarlett
text: Sometimes I catch Rhett watching me when he thinks I'm not looking. There's something in his face — love or grief, I can't tell which.

speaker: scarlett
text: I dream of Tara almost every night. Running through fog, trying to reach something I can never quite see. Rhett says it means I'm searching for safety. Maybe he's right.

speaker: scarlett
text: Bonnie rides her pony like Pa used to ride — reckless and laughing. It terrifies me but I can't bear to stop her joy.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett said he's taking Bonnie to London to make her a proper lady. As if Atlanta isn't good enough. As if I'm not good enough.

speaker: scarlett
text: I am lonely in a house full of servants and children. How did I end up with everything I wanted and nothing I need?

speaker: scarlett
text: The whole town is saying Bonnie is spoiled rotten. She is. But she has something I never had — a father who puts her first, always.

speaker: scarlett
text: I try to love my children the way Melanie loves her Beau — unconditionally, without agenda. But everything I do has an agenda. I don't know how to stop.

speaker: scarlett
text: I fell down the stairs and lost the baby. Rhett came to my bedside and cried. I've never seen Rhett Butler cry before.

speaker: scarlett
text: After the fall, something shifted between us. Like two people standing on opposite sides of a crack in the earth, watching it widen.

speaker: scarlett
text: I said terrible things to Rhett about not wanting his baby. I didn't mean them. I never mean the cruelest things I say. But by then the damage was done.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett is spending every night at Belle Watling's. Everyone knows. The humiliation burns, but what burns worse is that I miss him.

speaker: scarlett
text: India Wilkes saw me in Ashley's arms and now the whole town is talking. It wasn't what it looked like — I was just comforting him. But who will believe that?

speaker: scarlett
text: Ashley held me because I was crying and now they've turned it into a scandal. This town feeds on gossip the way I feed on money — hungrily and without shame.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett forced me to go to Ashley's party in that red dress. He wanted everyone to see me and judge me. He wanted to punish me in public.

speaker: scarlett
text: Melanie stood beside me at the party when everyone else turned away. She held my hand and dared anyone to say a word against me. That woman is iron wrapped in silk.

speaker: scarlett
text: I don't understand why Melanie defends me. After everything — stealing her brother-in-law's fiancee, throwing myself at her husband — she still calls me her dearest friend.

speaker: scarlett
text: Bonnie tried to jump a fence on her pony. The pony stumbled. My baby girl — my Bonnie — God, no. Not Bonnie.

speaker: scarlett
text: Bonnie is dead. My daughter is dead and Rhett has locked himself in her room and won't let them take her away. The sound of his grief is inhuman.

speaker: scarlett
text: I lost Bonnie the way Pa lost Mother — suddenly, completely. Now I understand his madness. Grief can break your mind as easily as your heart.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett blames himself for letting Bonnie jump. I want to comfort him but he won't let me near. The door between us isn't just locked — it's walled shut.

speaker: scarlett
text: Only Melanie could get Rhett to open that door. Only Melanie could reach through his grief with her gentle voice. I couldn't do what she did. I've never been gentle enough.

speaker: scarlett
text: Melanie is dying. She's calling for me and I'm running through the streets and all I can think is: not Melanie too. Please, not Melanie too.

speaker: scarlett
text: Melanie held my hand and told me to look after Ashley and Beau. Even dying, she thinks of others. I never learned how to do that.

speaker: scarlett
text: Melanie is dead. And suddenly I see — she was my sword and my shield. She stood between me and the whole world, and I never thanked her. Not once.

speaker: scarlett
text: I loved Melanie. I didn't know it until she was gone, but I loved her. She was the best friend I ever had and I treated her like a rival.

speaker: scarlett
text: Without Melanie, Ashley is just a man. A tired, sad, ordinary man. The golden light I saw around him was Melanie's light, reflected.

speaker: scarlett
text: I loved something I made up. I built a fantasy of Ashley in my mind and I wasted years of my life chasing a ghost.

speaker: scarlett
text: Ashley was never mine to love. He belonged to Melanie and to a world that doesn't exist anymore. I was in love with a dream.

speaker: scarlett
text: All this time, Rhett was the one. The one who saw me, challenged me, loved me despite everything. And I was too blind, too stubborn, too proud to see it.

speaker: scarlett
text: I ran home to tell Rhett I love him. Really love him — not the way I thought I loved Ashley, but truly, with my whole battered heart.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett, I love you. I know I've been terrible. I know I've been blind. But I love you and I want us to start over.

speaker: scarlett
text: He said he doesn't care anymore. After years of loving me, he's used it all up. There's nothing left. The cup is empty.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett is leaving me. He's really leaving. And I can't charm him or trick him or fight him into staying. For the first time, I have no strategy.

speaker: scarlett
text: Where will I go? What will I do? How do I live without the one person who ever truly knew me?

speaker: scarlett
text: I can't think about this now. If I think about it now, I'll go crazy. I'll think about it tomorrow.

speaker: scarlett
text: I'll go home to Tara. Tara has always healed me before. The red earth, the white columns — I'll find my strength there.

speaker: scarlett
text: I'll think of some way to get him back. I've never failed when I set my mind to something. Not at Tara, not in business, and not in this.

speaker: scarlett
text: After all, tomorrow is another day.
